food_name: Kangkung / Water Spinach (raw, leaves and tender stems)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for Ipomoea aquatica (water spinach / kangkung). The nearest parent is a leafy green vegetable. A new FoodOn concept is recommended for this major Southeast Asian vegetable.
ontology.foodex2_description: Water spinach / kangkung (Ipomoea aquatica), raw leaves and tender stems

classification.processing_level: unprocessed (raw leaves and tender stem tips)
main_ingredients: water spinach (Ipomoea aquatica), fresh raw leaves and tender stems

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard portion of kangkung is approximately 150g raw (before cooking). Kangkung wilts significantly when stir-fried — 150g raw yields approximately 100g cooked.

nutrient_source.title: Kangkung / Water Spinach (raw) — FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Food Composition Table; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore composition data
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table; Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo does not have a standalone raw kangkung entry (has cooked preparations). Values from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for water spinach (Ipomoea aquatica), raw, edible portion. Cross-referenced with available HPB data.

gi_source: GI not applicable — kangkung has negligible digestible carbohydrate (3.1g/100g including fibre). GI testing is not meaningful for low-carbohydrate leafy vegetables.

hypertension.key_factor: High potassium (312mg/100g) and very low calorie density. Good magnesium (71mg/100g). The sodium content (113mg/100g) is slightly elevated for a raw vegetable — this is natural sodium from the water in which it grows. The vitamin C (55mg/100g) supports vascular function.
hypertension.guidance: Kangkung is an excellent affordable leafy green for patients with hypertension in Singapore. Prepare kangkung belacan with minimal belacan (which adds sodium) — request reduced belacan/sauce versions at hawker stalls. Plain stir-fried kangkung with garlic is a better preparation for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low calorie (19 kcal/100g), negligible digestible carbohydrate. An affordable, widely available vegetable for increasing daily vegetable intake — critical for blood glucose management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kangkung is an ideal vegetable for patients with type 2 diabetes in Singapore — cheap, widely available at hawker centres and wet markets, very low in carbohydrates. Encourage daily consumption of 150–200g of kangkung or other leafy greens as a key dietary modification.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High vitamin C (55mg/100g) supports collagen synthesis and acts as an antioxidant. Good vitamin A (315mcg RAE/100g). Magnesium (71mg/100g) supports muscle and nerve function.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kangkung is a nutritionally valuable, affordable leafy green for patients with rheumatoid arthritis. Its high vitamin C content supports joint collagen integrity. The iron content (1.7mg/100g) is useful, though non-haem and needs to be consumed with vitamin C to maximise absorption — which kangkung naturally provides.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kangkung (Ipomoea aquatica, water spinach or morning glory) is the most commonly consumed leafy green vegetable in Singapore hawker cooking. It is a semi-aquatic plant native to Southeast Asia with hollow, arrow-shaped leaves and tender hollow stems. Extremely cheap and widely available at Singapore wet markets year-round. Very low in calories (19 kcal/100g) with high water content (92%). Rich in vitamin C (55mg/100g), vitamin A (315mcg RAE/100g from beta-carotene), and calcium (77mg/100g). The most iconic preparation is kangkung belacan (stir-fried with sambal, shrimp paste, and chilli) — a Singapore and Malaysian hawker staple. Also used in Assam laksa, char kway teow (sometimes), and Vietnamese soups.

confidence_reason: No standalone HPB Foodo raw kangkung entry. Values from FAO/INFOODS SEA Table for water spinach (Ipomoea aquatica) raw, cross-referenced with available HPB data for cooked kangkung dishes.
notes: Values per 100g raw kangkung leaves and tender stem tips. The sodium (113mg/100g) is naturally occurring from the aquatic growing environment — higher than most terrestrial vegetables. The vitamin C content (55mg/100g) is particularly notable and clinically useful for enhancing iron absorption from this and other non-haem iron sources consumed in the same meal.
